# Project-Local Command Shims

`boundary command install --project` creates project-local shims under
`.boundary/bin`.

```bash
boundary command install --project
```

Default shims:

```text
git
gh
rm
mv
cp
curl
wget
npm
pnpm
yarn
bun
node
python
python3
docker
kubectl
terraform
psql
```

Each shim is intentionally small:

```sh
#!/usr/bin/env sh
# Boundary project-local command shim.
# Generated by boundary command install --project.
exec boundary command run -- git "$@"
```

The command name changes per shim. For example, `.boundary/bin/docker` calls
`boundary command run -- docker "$@"`.

## Use Shims

Install does not modify `PATH` automatically. To use project shims in the
current shell, explicitly prepend `.boundary/bin`:

```bash
export PATH="$PWD/.boundary/bin:$PATH"
```

Or launch a scoped subshell:

```bash
boundary shell
```

## Remove Shims

```bash
boundary command uninstall --project
```

Uninstall removes only Boundary-generated shims. If an unrecognized file exists
under `.boundary/bin`, Boundary leaves it in place.

## No Global Mutation

Command Boundary preview does not edit:

```text
~/.zshrc
~/.bashrc
~/.profile
~/.config/fish/config.fish
global PATH
```

It is project-local and reversible by default.
